input.hypothesis: Funding + hiring surge + pricing repackage within a quarter suggests an expansion move.

facts.text: PayBridge announced a $40M Series C 'to accelerate enterprise expansion' (2026-05-02).
PayBridge posted 12 enterprise payments-engineering and enterprise-AE roles between 2026-05-20 and 2026-06-10.
PayBridge pricing page replaced the 'Scale' tier with a new 'Enterprise' tier with custom pricing and SSO/SLA add-ons (2026-06-18).

context.positioning: We sell billing infrastructure to mid-market SaaS; enterprise reliability is our wedge.

priorities.text: Defend the enterprise segment against PayBridge

input.hypothesis: Pricing change alongside hiring activity suggests a coordinated move.

facts.text: LedgerLy cut its Starter plan from $49 to $39/mo and removed seat minimums (2026-06-01).
LedgerLy posted 8 SMB-focused inside-sales roles in two weeks (2026-06-03 to 2026-06-17).

context.positioning: We win SMB self-serve billing on price and onboarding speed.

input.hypothesis: InvoiceHub's release cadence has accelerated far beyond its baseline.
input.stats: 9 changelog releases in 28 days vs 1.1 expected from baseline (p=0.0004, 3.3σ)

facts.text: InvoiceHub shipped usage-based billing (2026-06-02).
InvoiceHub shipped revenue-recognition reports (2026-06-11).
InvoiceHub shipped a Stripe-migration importer (2026-06-24).

context.positioning: We differentiate on breadth of billing features for usage-based SaaS.

priorities.text: Ship usage-based billing GA before InvoiceHub

input.hypothesis: A funding round, a hiring surge, and a pricing repackage within one quarter precede a launch or market entry within ~4 months.
input.trackRecord: Preceded 4 of 5 observed outcomes in the archive, typically 41–68 days ahead; 2 of 2 live predictions resolved as hits.

facts.text: PayBridge raised a $40M Series C (2026-05-02).
PayBridge posted 14 payments-infrastructure roles in six weeks (May–June 2026).
PayBridge repackaged pricing around a new Enterprise tier (2026-06-18).

context.positioning: We sell billing infrastructure to mid-market SaaS; enterprise reliability is our wedge.

priorities.text: Defend the enterprise segment against PayBridge

input.hypothesis: TallyForge's release cadence has shifted to a sustained higher level.
input.stats: 14 releases in 12 weeks vs 3.2 expected from baseline (p=0.0001, 3.7σ)

facts.text: TallyForge shipped multi-entity consolidation (2026-04-12).
TallyForge shipped audit-trail exports and SOC2 reporting (2026-05-05).
TallyForge shipped role-based approvals (2026-06-01).
TallyForge's changelog now ships weekly, up from roughly monthly across 2025.

context.positioning: We lead on enterprise-grade controls for finance teams.

input.hypothesis: Pricing activity precedes a launch within ~60 days.
input.trackRecord: Preceded 5 of 6 observed outcomes in the archive, typically 22–51 days ahead; 1 of 2 live predictions resolved as hits.

facts.text: MeterMint added a usage-metering add-on to its pricing page (2026-06-20).

context.positioning: We compete with MeterMint head-to-head on usage-based billing.
